title: andropogon saccharoides subsp. laguroides
intro: Andropogon saccharoides subsp. laguroides, commonly known as silver beard grass or broom grass, is a type of perennial grass native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The plant is believed to offer various health benefits, such as reducing fever, relieving respiratory issues, and providing antioxidant properties. Herbal preparations of the plant include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to treat a range of health conditions, although more research is needed to confirm its effectiveness.
